corrections.note: Need comma after σου: image reads 'ποιοῦντές σου, τὴν ἀλήθειαν'.
Image has grave accent: εὐοδία ἔσονται? Actually transcript should read εὐοδία ἔσονται with grave on final alpha: εὐοδία? Accent differs only if normalized; printed form appears εὐοδία.
Printed word is αὐλισθήτω with smooth breathing? transcript matches letters; correction is punctuation/layout: semicolon after αὐτῷ παραυτίκα, not middle dot.

corrections.location: first apparatus line, lemma before verse 6
corrections.note: Printed siglum is ℵ, not Hebrew א.
Printed apparatus reads πᾶσι, not πᾶσιν.
Replace Hebrew א with siglum ℵ in 'B*ℵA'.

corrections.correct: φοβου ποιειν] ου ποιησις sup ras Bab
corrections.note: Printed apparatus includes 'sup ras' after ποιησις.
Correct superscript is numeral 1, not c.
Printed forms are unaccented in apparatus; transcript should preserve that style consistently.
Printed apparatus reads ευλογηθησαν, without initial eta.

uncertain.note: Accentuation of εὐοδία/εὐοδία in print is hard to distinguish; likely not worth normalizing unless strict diplomatic accent capture is required.
Punctuation around 'παραυτίκα· ἐὰν δουλεύσῃς' appears as raised point/comma in scan; transcript may already be acceptable depending on house style.

notes: Running head and marginalia match overall. Main body is largely correct. Apparatus mostly correct but uses Hebrew א where printed siglum is ℵ, and omits 'sup ras' in line 8.
